thread.title: ‘He’s a monster’: Man pleads guilty to killing Hania Aguilar, gets life in prison
thread.title_full: ‘He’s a monster’: Man pleads guilty to killing Hania Aguilar, gets life in prison

author: @WTOC11
title: ‘He’s a monster’: Man pleads guilty to killing Hania Aguilar, gets life in prison
text:
‘He’s a monster’: Man pleads guilty to killing Hania Aguilar, gets life in prison
LUMBERTON, N.C. (WMBF) - A man will spend life behind bars after pleading guilty to the 2018 death of a Lumberton teenager.
MORE COVERAGE |
[Sheriff: Robeson County teen’s killer tried to escape before, after plea hearing](https://www.wmbfnews.com/2024/11/22/sheriff-robeson-county-teens-killer-tried-escape-before-after-plea-hearing/)
Michael McLellan pleaded guilty to first-degree murder charges in the death of 13-year-old Hania Aguilar on Friday.
Lumberton police said McLellan also pleaded guilty to charges in separate 2016 and 2018 attacks of two other, unrelated victims.
A judge then sentenced him to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
Officials added McLellan admitted to kidnapping Aguilar from her driveway on Nov. 5, 2018, before killing and disposing of her body.
Weeks after her disappearance, Aguilar’s body was found. An autopsy report shows that she died of “homicidal violence of undetermined means.”
Aguilar’s case has been the subject of national attention in the years since.
Friends and family of Aguilar were also at Friday’s hearing, with many saying justice has been a slow process filled with bittersweet emotions.
Others reflected on coming face to face with Aguilar’s now-convicted killer.
“I didn’t look him in the eyes,” said Emy Moscoso, a friend of Aguilar. “But I saw him sitting between his attorneys and I was thinking like ‘How could he have done that to an angel? To an innocent little girl? Harmless kid?’ I was asking all these questions, but I guess I will never get an answer because I don’t want to talk to him. He’s a monster.”
“Everyone in the Lumberton Police Department is humbled and grateful to bring justice for Hania, her family and this community,” Lumberton Police Chief Michael McNeill said in a statement. “This case has shown our relentless commitment and tireless efforts to hold violent criminals accountable for their actions. Before you go to bed tonight, please pray for Hania’s family to begin to heal their broken hearts.”
“The FBI has worked tirelessly from the day Hania was callously taken from outside her home to right now, when justice was served,” added Robert W. DeWitt, FBI Special Agent in Charge in North Carolina. “We hope Hania’s family and McLellan other victims are in some way comforted knowing he will spend the rest of his life in prison for his vicious crimes.”
